verses.latin_vulgate: Consurrexit autem Satan contra Israël, et concitavit David ut numeraret Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲמֹ֥ד שָׂטָ֖ן עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיָּ֙סֶת֙ אֶת־דָּוִ֔יד לִמְנ֖וֹת אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Satan rose up against Israel: and moved David to number Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja saatana seisoi Israelia vastaan ja kehoitti Davidin lukemaan Israelia.
verses.king_james: And Satan stood up against Israel, and provoked David to number Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque David ad Joab et ad principes populi : Ite, et numerate Israël a Bersabee usque Dan : et afferte mihi numerum ut sciam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֤יד אֶל־יוֹאָב֙ וְאֶל־שָׂרֵ֣י הָעָ֔ם לְכ֗וּ סִפְרוּ֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל מִבְּאֵ֥ר שֶׁ֖בַע וְעַד־דָּ֑ן וְהָבִ֣יאוּ אֵלַ֔י וְאֵדְעָ֖ה אֶת־מִסְפָּרָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Joab, and to the rulers of the people: Go, and number Israel from Bersabee even to Dan, and bring me the number of them that I may know it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi Joabille ja kansan ylimmäisille: menkäät ja lukekaat Israel Bersebasta Daniin saakka, ja tuokaat minulle, että minä tietäisin heidän lukunsa.
verses.king_james: And David said to Joab and to the rulers of the people, Go, number Israel from Beer–sheba even to Dan; and bring the number of them to me, that I may know it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responditque Joab : Augeat Dominus populum suum centuplum quam sunt : nonne, domine mi rex, omnes servi tui sunt ? quare hoc quærit dominus meus, quod in peccatum reputetur Israëli ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יוֹאָ֗ב יוֹסֵף֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה עַל־עַמּ֤וֹ ׀ כָּהֵם֙ מֵאָ֣ה פְעָמִ֔ים הֲלֹא֙ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ כֻּלָּ֥ם לַאדֹנִ֖י לַעֲבָדִ֑ים לָ֣מָּה יְבַקֵּ֥שׁ זֹאת֙ אֲדֹנִ֔י לָ֛מָּה יִֽהְיֶ֥ה לְאַשְׁמָ֖ה לְיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joab answered: The Lord make his people a hundred times more than they are: but, my lord the king, are they not all thy servants: why doth my lord seek this thing, which may be imputed as a sin to Israel?
verses.finnish_biblia: Joab sanoi: Herra enentäköön kansansa sata kertaa suuremmaksi kuin se nyt on. Herrani kuningas, eikö nämät kaikki ole herrani palveliat? miksi siis herrani kysyy sitä? miksi se tulis synniksi Israelille?
verses.king_james: And Joab answered, The Lord make his people an hundred times so many more as they be: but, my lord the king, are they not all my lord’s servants? why then doth my lord require this thing? why will he be a cause of trespass to Israel?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed sermo regis magis prævaluit : egressusque est Joab, et circuivit universum Israël : et reversus est Jerusalem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּדְבַר־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ חָזַ֣ק עַל־יוֹאָ֑ב וַיֵּצֵ֣א יוֹאָ֗ב וַיִּתְהַלֵּךְ֙ בְּכׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיָּבֹ֖א יְרוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the king's word rather prevailed: and Joab departed, and went through all Israel: and returned to Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuninkaan sana voitti Joabin. Ja Joab meni ja vaelsi koko Israelin ympäri, ja tuli Jerusalemiin.
verses.king_james: Nevertheless the king’s word prevailed against Joab. Wherefore Joab departed, and went throughout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: deditque David numerum eorum quos circuierat : et inventus est omnis numerus Israël, mille millia et centum millia virorum educentium gladium : de Juda autem quadringenta septuaginta millia bellatorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֥ן יוֹאָ֛ב אֶת־מִסְפַּ֥ר מִפְקַד־הָעָ֖ם אֶל־דָּוִ֑יד וַיְהִ֣י כׇֽל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֡ל אֶ֣לֶף אֲלָפִים֩ וּמֵאָ֨ה אֶ֤לֶף אִישׁ֙ שֹׁ֣לֵֽף חֶ֔רֶב וִיהוּדָ֕ה אַרְבַּע֩ מֵא֨וֹת וְשִׁבְעִ֥ים אֶ֛לֶף אִ֖ישׁ שֹׁ֥לֵֽף־חָֽרֶב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he gave David the number of them, whom he had surveyed: and all the number of Israel was found to be eleven hundred thousand men that drew the sword: and of Juda four hundred and seventy thousand fighting men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joab antoi Davidille luetun kansan luvun: ja koko Israelissa oli yksitoistakymmentä kertaa satatuhatta miestä, jotka miekkaa ulos vetivät. Ja Juudassa neljäsataa tuhatta ja seitsemänkymmentä tuhatta miekan vetävää miestä.
verses.king_james: And Joab gave the sum of the number of the people unto David. And all they of Israel were a thousand thousand and an hundred thousand men that drew sword: and Judah was four hundred threescore and ten thousand men that drew sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam Levi et Benjamin non numeravit : eo quod Joab invitus exsequeretur regis imperium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֵוִי֙ וּבִנְיָמִ֔ן לֹ֥א פָקַ֖ד בְּתוֹכָ֑ם כִּֽי־נִתְעַ֥ב דְּבַר־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אֶת־יוֹאָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Levi and Benjamin he did not number: for Joab unwillingly executed the king's orders.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Leviä ja BenJaminia ei hän näiden sekaan lukenut; sillä Joab kauhistui kuninkaan sanasta.
verses.king_james: But Levi and Benjamin counted he not among them: for the king’s word was abominable to Joab.

verses.latin_vulgate: Displicuit autem Deo quod jussum erat : et percussit Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֙רַע֙ בְּעֵינֵ֣י הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים עַל־הַדָּבָ֖ר הַזֶּ֑ה וַיַּ֖ךְ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And God was displeased with this thing that was commanded: and he struck Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta asia oli paha Jumalan silmäin edessä; ja hän löi Israelin.
verses.king_james: And God was displeased with this thing; therefore he smote Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque David ad Deum : Peccavi nimis ut hoc facerem : obsecro, aufer iniquitatem servi tui, quia insipienter egi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר דָּוִיד֙ אֶל־הָ֣אֱלֹהִ֔ים חָטָ֣אתִֽי מְאֹ֔ד אֲשֶׁ֥ר עָשִׂ֖יתִי אֶת־הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֑ה וְעַתָּ֗ה הַֽעֲבֶר־נָא֙ אֶת־עֲו֣וֹן עַבְדְּךָ֔ כִּ֥י נִסְכַּ֖לְתִּי מְאֹֽד׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to God: I have sinned exceedingly in doing this: I beseech thee take away the iniquity of thy servant, for I have done foolishly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi Jumalalle: minä olen raskaasti syntiä tehnyt, että minä sen tehnyt olen: mutta ota palvelias vääryys pois; sillä minä olen sangen tyhmästi tehnyt.
verses.king_james: And David said unto God, I have sinned greatly, because I have done this thing: but now, I beseech thee, do away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et locutus est Dominus ad Gad videntem Davidis, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְדַבֵּ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־גָּ֔ד חֹזֵ֥ה דָוִ֖יד לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord spoke to Gad the seer of David, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra puhui Gadille, Davidin näkiälle, sanoen:
verses.king_james: And the Lord spake unto Gad, David’s seer, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade, et loquere ad David, et dic ei : Hæc dicit Dominus : Trium tibi optionem do : unum, quod volueris, elige, et faciam tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵךְ֩ וְדִבַּרְתָּ֨ אֶל־דָּוִ֜יד לֵאמֹ֗ר כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה שָׁל֕וֹשׁ אֲנִ֖י נֹטֶ֣ה עָלֶ֑יךָ בְּחַר־לְךָ֛ אַחַ֥ת מֵהֵ֖נָּה וְאֶעֱשֶׂה־לָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go, and speak to David, and tell him: Thus saith the Lord: I give thee the choice of three things: choose one which thou wilt, and I will do it to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mene ja puhu Davidille, sanoen: näin sanoo Herra: kolme minä panen sinun etees: valitse yksi niistä, ja minä teen sen sinulle.
verses.king_james: Go and tell David, saying, Thus saith the Lord, I offer thee three things: choose thee one of them, that I may do it unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque venisset Gad ad David, dixit ei : Hæc dicit Dominus : Elige, quod volueris :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֥בֹא גָ֖ד אֶל־דָּוִ֑יד וַיֹּ֥אמֶר ל֛וֹ כֹּה־אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה קַבֶּל־לָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Gad was come to David, he said to him: Thus saith the Lord: choose which thou wilt:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Gad tuli Davidin tykö, sanoi hän hänelle: näin sanoo Herra: valitse sinulles:
verses.king_james: So Gad came to David, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Choose thee

verses.latin_vulgate: aut tribus annis famem ; aut tribus mensibus te fugere hostes tuos, et gladium eorum non posse evadere ; aut tribus diebus gladium Domini, et pestilentiam versari in terra, et angelum Domini interficere in universis finibus Israël : nunc igitur vide quid respondeam ei qui misit me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־שָׁל֨וֹשׁ שָׁנִ֜ים רָעָ֗ב וְאִם־שְׁלֹשָׁ֨ה חֳדָשִׁ֜ים נִסְפֶּ֥ה מִפְּנֵֽי־צָרֶ֘יךָ֮ וְחֶ֣רֶב אוֹיְבֶ֣יךָ ׀ לְמַשֶּׂ֒גֶת֒ וְאִם־שְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת יָ֠מִ֠ים חֶ֣רֶב יְהֹוָ֤ה וְדֶ֙בֶר֙ בָּאָ֔רֶץ וּמַלְאַ֣ךְ יְהֹוָ֔ה מַשְׁחִ֖ית בְּכׇל־גְּב֣וּל יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וְעַתָּ֣ה רְאֵ֔ה מָה־אָשִׁ֥יב אֶת־שֹׁלְחִ֖י דָּבָֽר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Either three years famine: or three months to flee from thy enemies, and not to be able to escape their sword: or three days to have the sword of the Lord, and pestilence in the land, and the angel of the Lord destroying in all the coasts of Israel: now therefore see what I shall answer him who sent me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Taikka kolme nälkävuotta, taikka kolmen kuukauden pako vihollistes edessä ja vainollises miekan edessä, että se on käsittävä sinun, eli kolmeksi päiväksi Herran miekka ja ruttotauti maalle, niin että Herran enkeli kadottaa kaikki Israelin rajoilla. Niin ajattele siis nyt, mitä minun pitää häntä vastaamaan, joka minun on lähettänyt.
verses.king_james: Either three years’ famine; or three months to be destroyed before thy foes, while that the sword of thine enemies overtaketh thee; or else three days the sword of the Lord, even the pestilence, in the land, and the angel of the Lord destroying throughout all the coasts of Israel. Now therefore advise thyself what word I shall bring again to him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit David ad Gad : Ex omni parte me angustiæ premunt : sed melius mihi est ut incidam in manus Domini, quia multæ sunt miserationes ejus, quam in manus hominum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר דָּוִ֛יד אֶל־גָּ֖ד צַר־לִ֣י מְאֹ֑ד אֶפְּלָה־נָּ֣א בְיַד־יְהֹוָ֗ה כִּֽי־רַבִּ֤ים רַחֲמָיו֙ מְאֹ֔ד וּבְיַד־אָדָ֖ם אַל־אֶפֹּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Gad: I am on every side in a great strait: but it is better for me to fall into the hands of the Lord, for his mercies are many, than into the hands of men.
verses.finnish_biblia: David sanoi Gadille: minulla on suuri ahdistus; kuitenkin tahdon minä langeta Herran käsiin, sillä hänen laupiutensa on sangen suuri, en tahdo langeta ihmisten käsiin.
verses.king_james: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait: let me fall now into the hand of the Lord; for very great are his mercies: but let me not fall into the hand of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit ergo Dominus pestilentiam in Israël : et ceciderunt de Israël septuaginta millia virorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֧ן יְהֹוָ֛ה דֶּ֖בֶר בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיִּפֹּל֙ מִיִּשְׂרָאֵ֔ל שִׁבְעִ֥ים אֶ֖לֶף אִֽישׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the Lord sent a pestilence upon Israel. And there fell of Israel seventy thousand men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra antoi ruttotaudin tulla Israeliin, niin että Israelista lankesi seitsemänkymmentä tuhatta miestä.
verses.king_james: So the Lord sent pestilence upon Israel: and there fell of Israel seventy thousand men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit quoque angelum in Jerusalem ut percuteret eam : cumque percuteretur, vidit Dominus, et misertus est super magnitudine mali : et imperavit angelo qui percutiebat : Sufficit, jam cesset manus tua. Porro angelus Domini stabat juxta aream Ornan Jebusæi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַח֩ הָאֱלֹהִ֨ים ׀ מַלְאָ֥ךְ ׀ לִֽירוּשָׁלַ֘͏ִם֮ לְהַשְׁחִיתָהּ֒ וּכְהַשְׁחִ֗ית רָאָ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ וַיִּנָּ֣חֶם עַל־הָרָעָ֔ה וַיֹּ֨אמֶר לַמַּלְאָ֤ךְ הַמַּשְׁחִית֙ רַ֔ב עַתָּ֖ה הֶ֣רֶף יָדֶ֑ךָ וּמַלְאַ֤ךְ יְהֹוָה֙ עֹמֵ֔ד עִם־גֹּ֖רֶן אׇרְנָ֥ן הַיְבוּסִֽי׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And he sent an angel to Jerusalem, to strike it: and as he was striking it, the Lord beheld, and took pity for the greatness of the evil: and said to the angel that destroyed: It is enough, now stop thy hand. And the angel of the Lord stood by the thrashingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jumala lähetti enkelin Jerusalemiin hukuttamaan sitä; ja hukuttaissa katsoi Herra siihen ja katui sitä pahaa, ja sanoi enkelille, joka hukutti: nyt on kyllä, pidä kätes ylös! Mutta Herran enkeli seisoi Ornanin Jebusilaisen riihen tykönä.
verses.king_james: And God sent an angel unto Jerusalem to destroy it: and as he was destroying, the Lord beheld, and he repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed, It is enough, stay now thine hand. And the angel of the Lord stood by the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levansque David oculos suos, vidit angelum Domini stantem inter cælum et terram, et evaginatum gladium in manu ejus, et versum contra Jerusalem : et ceciderunt tam ipse quam majores natu, vestiti ciliciis, proni in terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֨א דָוִ֜יד אֶת־עֵינָ֗יו וַיַּ֞רְא אֶת־מַלְאַ֤ךְ יְהֹוָה֙ עֹמֵ֗ד בֵּ֤ין הָאָ֙רֶץ֙ וּבֵ֣ין הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וְחַרְבּ֤וֹ שְׁלוּפָה֙ בְּיָד֔וֹ נְטוּיָ֖ה עַל־יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וַיִּפֹּ֨ל דָּוִ֧יד וְהַזְּקֵנִ֛ים מְכֻסִּ֥ים בַּשַּׂקִּ֖ים עַל־פְּנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David lifting up his eyes, saw the angel of the Lord standing between heaven and earth, with a drawn sword in his hand, turned against Jerusalem: and both he and the ancients clothed in haircloth, fell down flat on the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David nosti silmänsä ja sai nähdä Herran enkelin seisovan maan ja taivaan välillä, ja ulosvedetyn miekan hänen kädessänsä ojennetun Jerusalemin päälle. Niin lankesi David ja vanhimmat kasvoillensa, puetetut säkeillä.
verses.king_james: And David lifted up his eyes, and saw the angel of the Lord stand between the earth and the heaven, having a drawn sword in his hand stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders of Israel, who were clothed in sackcloth, fell upon their faces.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque David ad Deum : Nonne ego sum, qui jussi ut numeraretur populus ? ego, qui peccavi ? ego, qui malum feci ? iste grex, quid commeruit ? Domine Deus meus, vertatur, obsecro, manus tua in me, et in domum patris mei : populus autem tuus non percutiatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּוִ֣יד אֶֽל־הָאֱלֹהִ֡ים הֲלֹא֩ אֲנִ֨י אָמַ֜רְתִּי לִמְנ֣וֹת בָּעָ֗ם וַאֲנִי־ה֤וּא אֲשֶׁר־חָטָ֙אתִי֙ וְהָרֵ֣עַ הֲרֵע֔וֹתִי וְאֵ֥לֶּה הַצֹּ֖אן מֶ֣ה עָשׂ֑וּ יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהַ֗י תְּהִ֨י נָ֤א יָֽדְךָ֙ בִּ֚י וּבְבֵ֣ית אָבִ֔י וּֽבְעַמְּךָ֖ לֹ֥א לְמַגֵּפָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to God: Am not I he that commanded the people to be numbered? It is I that have sinned: it is I that have done the evil: but as for this flock, what hath it deserved? O Lord my God, let thy hand be turned, I beseech thee, upon me, and upon my father's house: and let not thy people be destroyed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi Jumalalle: enkö minä se ole, joka annoin lukea kansan? minä se olen, joka syntiä tein ja aivan pahoin tein. Mitä nämät lampaat tehneet ovat? Herra Jumalani, anna kätes olla minun ja minun isäni huoneen päälle, ja ei sinun kansalles vaivaksi.
verses.king_james: And David said unto God, Is it not I that commanded the people to be numbered? even I it is that have sinned and done evil indeed; but as for these sheep, what have they done? let thine hand, I pray thee, O Lord my God, be on me, and on my father’s house; but not on thy people, that they should be plagued.

verses.latin_vulgate: Angelus autem Domini præcepit Gad ut diceret Davidi ut ascenderet, exstrueretque altare Domino Deo in area Ornan Jebusæi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמַלְאַ֧ךְ יְהֹוָ֛ה אָמַ֥ר אֶל־גָּ֖ד לֵאמֹ֣ר לְדָוִ֑יד כִּ֣י ׀ יַעֲלֶ֣ה דָוִ֗יד לְהָקִ֤ים מִזְבֵּ֙חַ֙ לַֽיהֹוָ֔ה בְּגֹ֖רֶן אׇרְנָ֥ן הַיְבֻסִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the angel of the Lord commanded Gad to tell David, to go up, and build an altar to the Lord God in the thrashingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herran enkeli sanoi Gadille että hän sanois Davidille, että David menis ja rakentais Herralle alttarin Ornanin Jebusilaisen riiheen.
verses.king_james: Then the angel of the Lord commanded Gad to say to David, that David should go up, and set up an altar unto the Lord in the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendit ergo David juxta sermonem Gad, quem locutus ei fuerat ex nomine Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַל דָּוִיד֙ בִּדְבַר־גָּ֔ד אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֶּ֖ר בְּשֵׁ֥ם יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David went up, according to the word of Gad, which he spoke to him in the name of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David meni Gadin sanan jälkeen, jonka hän Herran nimeen puhunut oli.
verses.king_james: And David went up at the saying of Gad, which he spake in the name of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Ornan cum suspexisset et vidisset angelum, quatuorque filii ejus cum eo, absconderunt se : nam eo tempore terebat in area triticum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣שׇׁב אׇרְנָ֗ן וַיַּרְא֙ אֶת־הַמַּלְאָ֔ךְ וְאַרְבַּ֧עַת בָּנָ֛יו עִמּ֖וֹ מִֽתְחַבְּאִ֑ים וְאׇרְנָ֖ן דָּ֥שׁ חִטִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now when Ornan looked up, and saw the angel, he and his four sons hid themselves: for at that time he was thrashing wheat in the floor.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan kuin Ornan käänsi itsensä ja näki neljän poikansa kanssa enkelin, lymyttivät he heitänsä; sillä Ornan tappoi nisuja.
verses.king_james: And Ornan turned back, and saw the angel; and his four sons with him hid themselves. Now Ornan was threshing wheat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur cum veniret David ad Ornan, conspexit eum Ornan, et processit ei obviam de area, et adoravit eum pronus in terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֥א דָוִ֖יד עַד־אׇרְנָ֑ן וַיַּבֵּ֤ט אׇרְנָן֙ וַיַּ֣רְא אֶת־דָּוִ֔יד וַיֵּצֵא֙ מִן־הַגֹּ֔רֶן וַיִּשְׁתַּ֧חוּ לְדָוִ֛יד אַפַּ֖יִם אָֽרְצָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And as David was coming to Ornan, Ornan saw him, and went out of the thrashingfloor to meet him, and bowed down to him with his face to the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin David meni Ornanin tykö, näki Ornan ja havaitsi Davidin, ja meni riihestä ulos ja lankesi kasvoillensa Davidin eteen maahan.
verses.king_james: And as David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David, and went out of the threshingfloor, and bowed himself to David with his face to the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei David : Da mihi locum areæ tuæ, ut ædificem in ea altare Domino : ita ut quantum valet argenti accipias, et cesset plaga a populo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר דָּוִ֜יד אֶל־אׇרְנָ֗ן תְּנָה־לִּי֙ מְק֣וֹם הַגֹּ֔רֶן וְאֶבְנֶה־בּ֥וֹ מִזְבֵּ֖חַ לַֽיהֹוָ֑ה בְּכֶ֤סֶף מָלֵא֙ תְּנֵ֣הוּ לִ֔י וְתֵעָצַ֥ר הַמַּגֵּפָ֖ה מֵעַ֥ל הָעָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to him: Give me this place of thy thrashingfloor, that I may build therein an altar to the Lord: but thou shalt take of me as much money as it is worth, that the plague may cease from the people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi Ornanille: anna minulle sen riihen sia, rakentaakseni siihen Herralle alttarin; anna se minulle täyden rahan edestä, että vitsaus lakkais kansasta.
verses.king_james: Then David said to Ornan, Grant me the place of this threshingfloor, that I may build an altar therein unto the Lord: thou shalt grant it me for the full price: that the plague may be stayed from the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Ornan ad David : Tolle, et faciat dominus meus rex quodcumque ei placet : sed et boves do in holocaustum, et tribulas in ligna, et triticum in sacrificium : omnia libens præbebo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אׇרְנָ֤ן אֶל־דָּוִיד֙ קַֽח־לָ֔ךְ וְיַ֛עַשׂ אֲדֹנִ֥י הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ הַטּ֣וֹב בְּעֵינָ֑יו רְאֵה֩ נָתַ֨תִּי הַבָּקָ֜ר לָעֹל֗וֹת וְהַמּוֹרִגִּ֧ים לָעֵצִ֛ים וְהַחִטִּ֥ים לַמִּנְחָ֖ה הַכֹּ֥ל נָתָֽתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ornan said to David: Take it, and let my lord the king do all that pleaseth him: and moreover the oxen also I give for a holocaust, and the drays for wood, and the wheat for the sacrifice: I will give it all willingly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ornan sanoi Davidille: ota sinulles ja tee, herrani kuningas, kuin sinulle kelpaa. Katso, minä annan myös härkiä polttouhriksi ja aseita puiksi, ja nisuja ruokauhriksi: kaikki minä annan.
verses.king_james: And Ornan said unto David, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do that which is good in his eyes: lo, I give thee the oxen also for burnt offerings, and the threshing instruments for wood, and the wheat for the meat offering; I give it all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei rex David : Nequaquam ita fiet, sed argentum dabo quantum valet : neque enim tibi auferre debeo, et sic offerre Domino holocausta gratuita.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֤לֶךְ דָּוִיד֙ לְאׇרְנָ֔ן לֹ֕א כִּֽי־קָנֹ֥ה אֶקְנֶ֖ה בְּכֶ֣סֶף מָלֵ֑א כִּ֠י לֹא־אֶשָּׂ֤א אֲשֶׁר־לְךָ֙ לַיהֹוָ֔ה וְהַעֲל֥וֹת עוֹלָ֖ה חִנָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And king David said to him: It shall not be so, but I will give thee money as much as it is worth: for I must not take it from thee, and so offer to the Lord holocausts free cost.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuningas David sanoi Ornanille: ei niin, vaan minä tahdon peräti ostaa sen täydellä rahalla; sillä en minä ota sitä, mikä sinun on, Herralle ja tee polttouhria maksamata.
verses.king_james: And king David said to Ornan, Nay; but I will verily buy it for the full price: for I will not take that which is thine for the Lord, nor offer burnt offerings without cost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedit ergo David Ornan pro loco siclos auri justissimi ponderis sexcentos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֥ן דָּוִ֛יד לְאׇרְנָ֖ן בַּמָּק֑וֹם שִׁקְלֵ֣י זָהָ֔ב מִשְׁקָ֖ל שֵׁ֥שׁ מֵאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: So David gave to Ornan for the place, six hundred sicles of gold of just weight.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin David antoi Ornanille sen sian edestä kuudensadan siklin painon kultaa.
verses.king_james: So David gave to Ornan for the place six hundred shekels of gold by weight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ædificavit ibi altare Domino, obtulitque holocausta et pacifica, et invocavit Dominum ; et exaudivit eum in igne de cælo super altare holocausti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֩בֶן֩ שָׁ֨ם דָּוִ֤יד מִזְבֵּ֙חַ֙ לַֽיהֹוָ֔ה וַיַּ֥עַל עֹל֖וֹת וּשְׁלָמִ֑ים וַיִּקְרָא֙ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֔ה וַֽיַּעֲנֵ֤הוּ בָאֵשׁ֙ מִן־הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם עַ֖ל מִזְבַּ֥ח הָעֹלָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And he built there an altar to the Lord: and he offered holocausts, and peace offerings, and he called upon the Lord, and he heard him by sending fire from heaven upon the altar of the holocaust.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David rakensi siinä Herralle alttarin ja uhrasi polttouhria ja kiitosuhria, ja rukoili Herraa; ja hän kuuli hänen tulella taivaasta polttouhrin alttarilla.
verses.king_james: And David built there an altar unto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings, and called upon the Lord; and he answered him from heaven by fire upon the altar of burnt offering.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præcepitque Dominus angelo, et convertit gladium suum in vaginam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ לַמַּלְאָ֔ךְ וַיָּ֥שֶׁב חַרְבּ֖וֹ אֶל־נְדָנָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord commanded the angel: and he put up his sword again into the sheath.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi enkelille, että hän pistäis miekkansa tuppeensa.
verses.king_james: And the Lord commanded the angel; and he put up his sword again into the sheath thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Protinus ergo David, videns quod exaudisset eum Dominus in area Ornan Jebusæi, immolavit ibi victimas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּעֵ֣ת הַהִ֔יא בִּרְא֤וֹת דָּוִיד֙ כִּֽי־עָנָ֣הוּ יְהֹוָ֔ה בְּגֹ֖רֶן אׇרְנָ֣ן הַיְבוּסִ֑י וַיִּזְבַּ֖ח שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David seeing that the Lord had heard him in the thrashingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite, forthwith offered victims there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin David näki Herran kuulleeksi häntä Ornanin Jebusilaisen riihessä, uhrasi hän siellä.
verses.king_james: At that time when David saw that the Lord had answered him in the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite, then he sacrificed there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tabernaculum autem Domini, quod fecerat Moyses in deserto, et altare holocaustorum, ea tempestate erat in excelso Gabaon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִשְׁכַּ֣ן יְ֠הֹוָ֠ה אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֨ה מֹשֶׁ֧ה בַמִּדְבָּ֛ר וּמִזְבַּ֥ח הָעוֹלָ֖ה בָּעֵ֣ת הַהִ֑יא בַּבָּמָ֖ה בְּגִבְעֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses made in the desert, and the altar of holocausts, was at that time in the high place of Gabaon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herran maja, jonka Moses oli tehnyt korvessa, ja polttouhrin alttari olivat silloin Gibeonin korkeudella.
verses.king_james: For the tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses made in the wilderness, and the altar of the burnt offering, were at that season in the high place at Gibeon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non prævaluit David ire ad altare ut ibi obsecraret Deum : nimio enim fuerat in timore perterritus, videns gladium angeli Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־יָכֹ֥ל דָּוִ֛יד לָלֶ֥כֶת לְפָנָ֖יו לִדְרֹ֣שׁ אֱלֹהִ֑ים כִּ֣י נִבְעַ֔ת מִפְּנֵ֕י חֶ֖רֶב מַלְאַ֥ךְ יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And David could not go to the altar there to pray to God: for he was seized with an exceeding great fear, seeing the sword of the angel of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ei David taitanut mennä sen eteen kysymään Jumalaa; sillä hän oli peljästynyt Herran enkelin miekkaa.
verses.king_james: But David could not go before it to enquire of God: for he was afraid because of the sword of the angel of the Lord.
